decks.notes: This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and direct damage spells. The strategy revolves around putting pressure on the opponent early in the game with efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ocelot Pride, while also using burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to finish off the opponent or deal with problematic creatures. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory provide additional threats and support for the aggressive game plan. The mana base is designed to support a fast start and consistent mana base for spells.

The deck focuses on generating mana quickly through rituals and medallions to cast powerful spells like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Past in Flames multiple times in a turn. It utilizes card draw spells like Reckless Impulse and Valakut Awakening to find combo pieces and digs deep into the deck with Glimpse the Impossible. The deck uses Ral's abilities and cards like Grapeshot and Strike It Rich for finishers, while also having Wish to access sideboard cards in a pinch. The lands are optimized for red mana sources and some utility lands for flexibility.

The main concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly flood the board with aggressive creatures and deal damage to the opponent as quickly as possible. With cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer leading the charge, backed up by removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast and relentless attacks. Seasoned Pyromancer and Guide of Souls provide additional value and card advantage, while cards like Fable of the Mirror-Breaker and Voice of Victory offer additional support and flexibility in different game situations.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ocelot Pride, supported by burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory provide additional support and buffs for the creatures, while cards like Seasoned Pyromancer and Fable of the Mirror-Breaker provide card advantage and flexibility. The mana base is optimized for consistency and efficiency, with Arid Mesa and Marsh Flats fetching the necessary colors, and Arena of Glory providing additional utility. Overall, this deck aims to win fast through aggressive plays and efficient removal.

This deck focuses on aggressive creatures and burn spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It also utilizes Leyline cards to disrupt the opponent's strategy and control the board. The deck has a diverse mana base consisting of multiple colors to support its powerful spells and creatures. Overall, the strategy of this deck is to apply pressure early on and maintain control of the game through a mix of creature aggression and spell disruption.

The main theme of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's strategy through a combination of countering spells, removal, and hand disruption while also maintaining card advantage with cards like Murktide Regent and Psychic Frog. The deck aims to control the game through a mix of tempo plays and powerful threats, all while keeping the opponent on the back foot and limiting their options. With a focus on resource denial and strong control elements, this deck seeks to outlast the opponent and secure victory through careful manipulation of the game state.

The main idea of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and direct damage spells while utilizing cards that can be played quickly and efficiently to maintain a high tempo. The deck focuses on using cards like Burning-Tree Emissary and Vengevine to quickly build a board presence and pressure the opponent. Cards like Mishra's Bauble and Faithless Looting are included to help with card advantage and selection. Overall, the deck's strategy is to apply consistent pressure and finish off the opponent quickly before they have a chance to stabilize.

This deck focuses on using cheap creatures with the Delirium mechanic to quickly put pressure on the opponent. By discarding cards and cycling through the deck, it aims to consistently bring out powerful threats like Hollow One and Vengevine early in the game. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Lightning Axe and Burning Inquiry to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy while maintaining card advantage through card draw and discard effects.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana with land-bouncing effects and generating large amounts of mana through Amulet of Vigor and Urza's Saga. The key strategy is to use Primeval Titan to fetch powerful lands like Boseiju, Who Endures and Lotus Field, enabling the player to cast game-winning spells like Scapeshift and Cultivator Colossus. The deck's combo potential is further enhanced with cards like Arboreal Grazer and Green Sun's Zenith, allowing for explosive plays and overwhelming the opponent with powerful creatures and game-ending spells.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures and direct damage spells. The deck aims to control the board early with aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ocelot Pride, while also utilizing burn spells like Galvanic Discharge to chip away at the opponent's life total. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provides additional creature support and card advantage, while cards like Thraben Charm and Goblin Bombardment offer removal options. The deck's mana base is optimized for consistent access to its spells, allowing for a fast and aggressive playstyle.

The main concept of the deck is to control the game by using a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining card advantage through cards like Wan Shi Tong, Librarian and Snapcaster Mage. It also utilizes strong control cards like Subtlety and Sheoldred's Edict to deal with threats efficiently. The mana base is designed to support the deck's control elements, with Field of Ruin for land destruction and a mix of fetch lands and shock lands for color fixing.

This deck focuses on aggressive tactics combined with blinking effects to maximize the value of creatures entering and leaving the battlefield. The strategy involves playing efficient threats like Phelia and Phlage, while using Quantum Riddler for card advantage. Consign to Memory and Galvanic Discharge offer removal options, while Solitude provides protection. With cards like Ephemerate and Teferi, Time Raveler, the deck can repeatedly blink its creatures, disrupting the opponent's strategy. The mana base is designed for consistency and flexibility, allowing the deck to adapt to different matchups effectively.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo using Hydroelectric Specimen, Tameshi, Reality Architect, and Thundertrap Trainer to generate a winning advantage. By utilizing cards like Goblin Charbelcher and Lotus Bloom alongside counter spells like Disrupting Shoal and Spell Snare, the deck aims to disrupt opponents while setting up its own win condition. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Whir of Invention and Waterlogged Teachings provides the deck with flexibility and consistency in executing its game plan. The deck's Landless nature allows for smooth gameplay without relying heavily on traditional land cards.

The deck focuses on using a combination of fast mana generation, card draw, and spell recursion to quickly assemble a powerful win condition in Goblin Charbelcher. By utilizing spells like Desperate Ritual and Manamorphose to generate mana and Irencrag Feat to potentially cast multiple spells in a turn, the deck aims to cast Goblin Charbelcher and activate its ability to deal massive damage to the opponent. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Orim's Chant and removal in cards like Sundering Eruption to control the board and protect the combo.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo using Hydroelectric Specimen, Tameshi, Reality Architect, and Thundertrap Trainer to generate mana and deal damage. By using cards like Whir of Invention and Waterlogged Teachings to search for key pieces, the deck aims to win the game quickly without needing lands. Disrupting Shoal, Spell Snare, and Force of Negation provide countermagic to protect the combo, while cards like Sink into Stupor and Stern Scolding disrupt the opponent's game plan. Lotus Bloom helps ramp into the combo, with various other utility cards rounding out the strategy.
